site.title: Jane Smith for City Council
site.metaDescription: Jane Smith is running for City Council — fighting for better schools, safer neighborhoods, and a stronger community. Learn more and get involved.

candidate.name: Jane Smith
candidate.tagline: Building a stronger community for every family.

about.title: About Jane
paragraphs: Jane Smith has lived in District 5 for over 15 years. A public school parent, small business owner, and longtime community volunteer, she knows firsthand the challenges facing local families — from overcrowded classrooms to aging infrastructure.

After serving on the PTA, leading the neighborhood association, and organizing the annual community cleanup for the past eight years, Jane decided it was time to bring her passion for problem-solving to City Hall.

Jane holds a degree in Public Administration from State University and spent a decade in nonprofit management before starting her own consulting firm. She lives in Maplewood with her husband, two kids, and a very opinionated rescue dog named Biscuit.

items.title: Better Schools
items.description: Every child in our district deserves a quality education.
items.detail: Jane will fight for fair school funding, smaller class sizes, and up-to-date facilities and technology. Her plan includes advocating for a 15% increase in per-pupil funding, expanding after-school programs, and ensuring every school building meets modern safety and accessibility standards.

items.title: Public Safety
items.description: Safer neighborhoods start with community-based solutions.
items.detail: Jane supports investing in community policing, better street lighting, and youth diversion programs. She will push for a dedicated neighborhood liaison officer program and increased funding for mental health crisis response teams that work alongside first responders.

items.title: Affordable Housing
items.description: Working families shouldn't be priced out of our community.
items.detail: Jane will push for affordable housing policies that keep long-time residents in their homes. She supports inclusionary zoning, expanding the community land trust, and creating a tenant protection ordinance to prevent unjust evictions and rent gouging.

items.title: Parks & Green Space
items.description: Our parks and trails are community treasures.
items.detail: Jane will champion increased park maintenance funding and new green spaces in underserved areas. Her vision includes a new community garden program, restoring the Riverside Trail, and planting 1,000 trees in District 5 over the next four years.

items.title: Transportation
items.description: Getting around our district should be easy and affordable.
items.detail: Jane supports expanded bus routes, safer bike lanes, and fixing our roads and sidewalks. Priorities include extending the Route 7 bus line to cover the south side, adding protected bike lanes on Main Street, and a comprehensive pothole repair initiative.

items.title: Local Economy
items.description: Small businesses are the backbone of District 5.
items.detail: Jane will advocate for reducing red tape, supporting local entrepreneurs, and attracting good-paying jobs. She will work to establish a small business incubator, streamline the permitting process, and create a "Buy Local" initiative to support neighborhood businesses.

items.quote: Jane is exactly the kind of leader District 5 needs — someone who listens, shows up, and gets things done. She has my full support.
items.name: Mayor Robert Chen
items.title: Mayor, City of Springfield

items.quote: As a fellow parent and educator, I know Jane will always put our children's futures first. She's been a tireless advocate for our schools for years.
items.name: Dr. Maria Garcia
items.title: Superintendent, Springfield Schools

items.quote: Jane understands the needs of small business owners because she is one. She'll bring a practical, results-oriented approach to city government.
items.name: Tom Nguyen
items.title: President, District 5 Business Association

items.quote: I've worked alongside Jane for years in our neighborhood association. Her dedication to this community is genuine, and her energy is unmatched.
items.name: Sarah Johnson
items.title: Maplewood Neighborhood Association

items.quote: Jane has been a champion for green spaces and environmental sustainability in our district.
items.name: David Park
items.title: Director, Springfield Environmental Coalition

items.quote: As a union member and working parent, I know Jane will fight for fair wages and working conditions. She's one of us.
items.name: Lisa Martinez
items.title: Local 302, Service Workers Union

volunteer.title: Get Involved
volunteer.description: This campaign is powered by people like you. Whether you can knock on doors, make phone calls, host a yard sign, or spread the word — every bit of help makes a difference.

donate.title: Support the Campaign
donate.description: Grassroots donations power this campaign. Every dollar helps us reach more voters, print more signs, and fight for the issues that matter to District 5 families. No amount is too small.

items.title: Community Town Hall
items.description: Join Jane for an open town hall to discuss the issues that matter most to District 5.

items.title: Neighborhood Canvass Kickoff
items.description: Help us knock on doors and talk to voters! Training provided.

items.title: Fundraising Dinner
items.description: An evening of food, community, and conversation.

items.title: Park Cleanup & Meet and Greet
items.description: Roll up your sleeves and join Jane for a community cleanup.
